description_long: This house features a prominent stone chimney and a steeply pitched gable roof, giving it a classic, old-world appearance. The facade is characterized by a large, multi-pane window section on the upper floor, supported by dark timber framing, suggesting Tudor or medieval influences. The stonework is rough and gray, contrasting with the dark wood elements and the shingled roof. The side elevations reveal the chimney running the full height of the building and a relatively narrow footprint. The rear and side views show fewer windows and a more utilitarian design, with the stone chimney as a dominant vertical element. The overall structure appears compact, with a single main entry likely located beneath the overhanging upper story.
description: A compact, stone-and-timber house with a steep gable roof, large upper windows, and a prominent stone chimney, evoking a Tudor or medieval style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the large multi-pane window, dark timber framing, and the stone chimney on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep gable roof, the full height of the stone chimney, and the narrow profile of the house.
description_view_2: Rear view with the stone chimney now on the left, minimal window openings, and a clear view of the shingled roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the gable end, the stone chimney running up the center, and the narrow width of the structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large upper-story windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry beneath overhanging upper story, likely with a simple wooden door and minimal decorative elements.
